channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: College star Luke Clanton easily makes the cut in Cognizant Classic and earns PGA Tour card
item.description: PALM BEACH GARDENS, Fla. (AP) — Florida State junior Luke Clanton only needed to make the cut Friday in the Cognizant Classic to earn a PGA Tour card. That was never in doubt.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Riley Gaines urges AOC to change position on trans athletes in women's sports amid Rep's recent silence on it
item.description: Amid Alexandria Ocasion-Cortez's recent silence on the trans athlete debate, Riley Gaines has invited the congresswomen to switch sides on the issue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: QB Shedeur Sanders uses vast array of football resources to emerge as possible No. 1 draft pick
item.description: Quarterback Shedeur Sanders used everything at his disposal to finetune his game — six college offensive coordinators at two different schools, a multitude of NFL resources, his own father, even Tom Brady.
item.dc:creator: Michael Marot, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Joel Embiid will miss the rest of the season for treatment and rehabilitation of his left knee
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — Joel Embiid will miss the rest of the season, with the Philadelphia 76ers saying Friday he is “medically unable to play” and will focus on treatment and rehabilitation of his left knee.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reese, Clark to meet in bigger arena when Sky host Fever, with games moved to United Center
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Angel Reese and Caitlin Clark will meet in a bigger venue when the Chicago Sky host the Indiana Fever this season, with the games being moved from Wintrust Arena to the United Center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards suspended 1 game by NBA after picking up 16th technical foul
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Minnesota Timberwolves star Anthony Edwards was suspended one game without pay by the NBA on Friday after picking up his 16th technical foul of the season during the third quarter against the Los Angeles Lakers.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: QB Matthew Stafford gets a restructured contract and agrees to stay with the Los Angeles Rams
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Quarterback Matthew Stafford is staying with the Los Angeles Rams under a restructured contract, ending weeks of speculation about the Super Bowl winner's future. The Rams announced their new agreement with Stafford on Friday without initially revealing any details.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mailbag: Conference merger scenarios, an MLB model, the poaching-penalty lawsuit, Notre Dame’s status and more
item.description: Depending on the next move by the SEC and Big Ten, or the formation of a super league, there could be merger options for other conferences.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Rams, Matthew Stafford reach agreement to keep quarterback in Los Angeles
item.description: The Los Angeles Rams and star quarterback Matthew Stafford agreed to a restructured deal to keep Stafford in Los Angeles, the team announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia passes 'Riley Gaines Act' that aims to prohibit trans athletes from competing in female sports
item.description: The Georgia House passed the "Riley Gaines Act" on Thursday, which aims to keep transgender athletes from competing against biological girls and women.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police justified in use of pepper spray during Ohio State-Michigan brawl: report
item.description: Police were reportedly justified in their use of pepper spray during the brawl between Michigan and Ohio State players in November.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Monterey County soccer coach pleads guilty to 12 counts of crimes against children
item.description: Julio Cesar Gonzalez Garcia pleaded guilty to 3 charges of contacting children online for the purpose of distributing sexual material, 1 charge for providing marijuana to a child and 8 charges of unlawful sexual touching including forcible lewd acts.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Valadez, The Mercury News

item.title: Stephen Curry does it all in a 56-point masterpiece, from a halfcourt heave to a jersey toss to mom
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — When Stephen Curry has it going from long range like he did Thursday night — like he has more often than any player in NBA history — his coach just knows the ball is going in.
item.dc:creator: DICK SCANLON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Davis beats Herriman to win 6A state title
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - In a battle of the top two 6A boys basketball teams, Davis proved to be the best in the state. Coleman Atwater poured in 24 points, while Bode Sparrow added 16 as the #2 seeded Darts knocked off top-seeded Herriman, 75-66.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Travis Kelce's podcast confirms his NFL return with nod to legendary 'Wolf of Wall Street' scene
item.description: Travis Kelce's "New Heights" podcast announced the tight end would return for another NFL season, posting a clip from "Wolf of Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Criticism of Wayne Gretzky by Canadians due to his support for Trump has 'broken his heart,' wife says
item.description: Wayne Gretzky's wife, Janet, said the criticism he has received from Canadians has "broken his heart" amid is support for President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Buccaneers announce Jon Gruden will be reinstated into Ring of Honor
item.description: Jon Gruden will be reinstated in the Tampa Bay Buccaneers' Ring of Honor, the team announced Thursday. Gruden was removed following his 2021 email scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area rap legend to perform at Oakland Roots opener
item.description: (KRON) -- Bay Area rap icon Too Short has been announced as the halftime entertainment for the Oakland Roots Soccer Club's home opener next month. The Roots will play their 2025 season opener on Saturday, March 22 against San Antonio FC.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Bears prospects to watch at the NFL Scouting Combine: Defensive linemen and linebackers
item.description: The NFL Scouting Combine is showcasing top defensive line and linebacker prospects, including James Pearce Jr., Mykel Williams, Mike Green, Shemar Stewart, Jack Sawyer, Donovan Ezeiruaku, Landon Jackson, Princely Umanmielen, Mason Graham, and Kenneth Grant.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Boys Basketball: Carlsbad 73, Cathedral 61
item.description:
Carlsbad basketball had a repeat on its mind: a goal to get back to the Open Division title game.
Consider it mission accomplished after a 73 to 61 win over a hard charging Cathedral team.
Jett Kenady was the difference for the Lancers on Tuesday night, scoring 24 points. Jake Hall added 22.
item.dc:creator: Brandon Stone, Fox 5

item.title: NFLPA Survey: Matt Eberflus rated worst head coach in 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The NFL Players Association released the results of their annual players survey Wednesday at the NFL Scouting Combine.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: GJ River Hawks 19U girls team set for first-ever playoffs at River City Sportplex
item.description: River City Sportplex is currently in the midst of the first-ever GJ River Hawks’ 19U Girls hockey season. Now, it's playoff time.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Poles, Johnson detail 'clear vision' for Bears heading into NFL Scouting Combine
item.description: The Chicago Bears are looking to upgrade their offensive line and add a pass rusher in the upcoming NFL Scouting Combine, while also evaluating their running back and tight end positions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz late comeback falls short against Portland
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Anfernee Simons had 28 points and out six assists to lead the Portland Trail Blazers to a 114-112 victory over the Utah Jazz on Monday night. Keyonte George, who scored 21 points, missed a 25-foot three-pointer at the buzzer that would have won the game.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Guenther scores game-winner in 2-1 victory over Vancouver
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Dylan Guenther scored the tiebreaking goal with 5:53 left in regulation, and the Utah Hockey Club defeated the Vancouver Canucks 2-1 at Delta Center on Sunday. Guenther scored on the power play by tipping in a backdoor pass from Nick Schmaltz at the left post.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Utah's comeback effort falls short at UCF, 76-72
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (ABC4 Sports) - Darius Johnson scored a reverse layup with 2.5 seconds left to give UCF the lead and Keyshawn Hall added two free throws after a turnover as the Knights knocked off Utah, 76-72 Sunday to snap a seven-game losing streak and earn their first win in a month.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Hayton records first Utah hat trick in 5-3 loss to Kings
item.description: LOS ANGELES (ABC4 Sports) - Despite Barrett Hayton recording the first hat trick in Utah Hockey Club history, Drew Doughty had a goal and two assists in the Los Angeles Kings’ 5-3 win at Crypto.com Arena on Saturday. “I thought we kind of left it all out there, played with good pace,” Hayton said.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
